meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits for Freelancers: Scale Your Development
content.seo_description: Stop reinventing the wheel. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your freelance projects and increase your profit margins.
content.answer_block: The bottleneck in freelance development is manual configuration. By leveraging a production-ready SaaS starter kit, you unify your stack, automate boilerplate, and ship features 5x faster. Using Antigravity to structure the architecture and Claude Code to implement business logic allows you to move from setup to deployment in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the client project is greenlit, the excitement is high, and then you spend three days wrestling with authentication, database migrations, and environment variables. You aren't building a product; you're building a foundation that you've already built a dozen times before. This is the 'freelancer's trap'—the inability to scale because every project is a bespoke disaster waiting to happen.

But what if you could treat your codebase like a product line? By adopting a modular SaaS starter kit, you shift from being a manual laborer to an architectural lead. This isn't just about speed; it's about repeatability and profit margins. Let’s strip away the friction and build a system that works for you, not the other way around.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Establish a consistent folder structure and base configuration that matches your preferred tech stack (e.g., Next.js, Postgres, Prisma).
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-grade SaaS boilerplate structure including authentication, database schema with Prisma, and standardized API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project with standard SaaS folder structure'

steps.title: Automate Authentication Logic
steps.description: Don't write auth from scratch. Integrate a provider like Clerk or NextAuth into your starter kit to handle sessions securely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Clerk authentication middleware and protect API routes in the new project structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate Clerk auth into existing project files'

steps.title: Define Database Schema Patterns
steps.description: Standardize your user and subscription models so they can be ported across any client project effortlessly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable Prisma schema for a SaaS app including User, Subscription, and Organization models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply schema.prisma updates to database'

steps.title: Implement Global UI Components
steps.description: Use a design system like Shadcn/UI to ensure your UI is consistent, accessible, and easily themeable for different client brands.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a library of essential UI components (Button, Input, Card, Modal) using Shadcn/UI conventions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'install and configure shadcn components'

steps.title: Deploy the Blueprint
steps.description: Finalize your CI/CD pipeline so every new project is one command away from a live staging URL.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Vercel deployment configuration file with environment variable validation for production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure Vercel pipeline and verify deployment'

pro_tips: Always store your 'base' starter kit in a private GitHub template repository for instant cloning.
Use environment variable validation libraries like T3-env to catch configuration errors before deployment.
Never hardcode business logic; use a service-layer pattern to keep your controllers lean and testable.
Pre-configure a 'mock' data generator in your kit to speed up frontend development without waiting for APIs.
Audit your starter kit every 90 days to update dependencies and remove deprecated patterns.
content.next_steps: Now that you have your starter kit, perform a 'dry run' by building a simple CRUD application to identify any missing abstractions, then move to your next client project with your new, optimized workflow.
content.conversion_block: Stop burning billable hours on setup. Master the art of the SaaS starter kit and reclaim your time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
